customManagers.description: `pip install poetry==X.Y.Z` style pins inside the Dockerfile (and any other shell context). Annotated with a `# renovate: datasource=pypi depName=<pkg>` comment directly above the install line. Renovate's dockerfile manager does not parse `pip install`, so without this customManager pinned versions of bootstrap CLIs (poetry, uv when invoked via pip, etc.) would silently drift.

Pinned `[*-]version: vX.Y.Z` inputs in GitHub Actions workflows (annotated with `# renovate: datasource=... depName=... [packageName=...] [versioning=...]`). Matches bare `version:` (e.g. setup-uv's uv CLI version) and toolchain-style `python-version:` / `node-version:` etc., but only when an annotation comment is attached on the preceding line — so unannotated matrix values stay invisible to Renovate.

packageRules.description: Supply-chain cooldown: wait 3 days before raising updates from public registries with reliable timestamps (pypi, github-tags, github-releases). Protects against short-lived malicious releases (e.g. ua-parser-js, event-stream). Security updates bypass this via `vulnerabilityAlerts`/OSV.
Same cooldown for Docker Hub images (other registries lack timestamps; `minimumReleaseAgeBehaviour: timestamp-required` handles those gracefully).
Auto-merge patch and minor bumps on stable (1.0+) packages. Majors and 0.x always go through manual review.
Weekly patch/minor group — Python deps (PEP 621, managed by uv).
Weekly patch/minor group — Docker images.
Weekly patch/minor group — CI (actions + pre-commit).

Group SQLAlchemy + Alembic + asyncpg together — Alembic pins a SQLAlchemy range, asyncpg ABI is exercised through SQLAlchemy's asyncio dialect, and partial bumps produce migration-env breakage and version-resolution noise.

Group the FastAPI server stack (FastAPI + uvicorn + gunicorn + httptools + ujson + starlette internals). FastAPI's Starlette/Pydantic compatibility window is narrow, gunicorn's UvicornWorker compatibility tracks uvicorn internals, and httptools is exercised through both — half-bumped stacks routinely surface as obscure ASGI lifecycle bugs.

Group Taskiq + plugins. Taskiq core defines the broker/scheduler/middleware contracts; `taskiq-redis` and `taskiq-fastapi` track those contracts and break at startup if the core jumps a minor without them.
Group Pydantic + pydantic-settings — pydantic-settings pins a pydantic range, and partial bumps produce import-time validator errors.

Group the pytest stack (pytest + pytest-* plugins + anyio + fakeredis + pytest-env). Plugin ABIs track pytest internals, and a half-bumped stack typically breaks collection (seen historically with pytest-asyncio / anyio).

Group yt-dlp ecosystem. `bgutil-ytdlp-pot-provider` is a yt-dlp plugin and tracks yt-dlp's plugin API; bumping yt-dlp without it can disable YouTube PO-token flow on the next release. yt-dlp itself releases very frequently (~weekly), so it skips the weekly-updates grouping and lands as its own PR.
Group the Instagram-related libraries — `instagrapi` and `instaloader` cover overlapping concerns (web scraping vs login session) and tend to break together when Instagram tightens auth.

Playwright bumps need a matching browser revision in the headless-Chrome container. Always route through the Dependency Dashboard so the docker-compose `chrome` image (currently `chromedp/headless-shell:latest`) can be updated in lockstep — Renovate cannot infer this dependency.
Python project commits land under the `deps` scope.
Docker scope (SHA pinning comes from `docker:pinDigests` in `config:best-practices`).
GitHub Actions scope (SHA pinning comes from `helpers:pinGitHubActionDigests` in `config:best-practices`).
pre-commit hooks — low-stakes chore bumps.

Do not touch the `requires-python` constraint — runtime interpreter choice is an environment decision (ties into the Dockerfile base image, CI matrix, and binary wheel availability), not a routine dep bump.

Do not auto-merge GitHub Actions updates. The Mend Renovate app has `workflows:write`; a compromised action (tj-actions/changed-files-style supply-chain hit) combined with auto-merge could exfiltrate secrets before review. PRs still open automatically so they show up in review queues — we just require a human to hit Merge. Placed AFTER the global auto-merge rule so this override wins by rule-order.
Major bumps on Docker images (Debian codename rollover, Postgres major, Python major, etc.) land via Dependency Dashboard approval rather than flooding PRs weekly.

Toolchain *runtime* bumps captured by the workflow `*-version:` customManager route through the Dependency Dashboard — matches depName `python` only (we have no Go/Node/Bun in this repo). These are environment decisions that need to stay in sync with `requires-python`, the Dockerfile base image, and the uv image tag; a silent weekly PR to python 3.15 would desync CI from prod. The `astral-sh/uv` CLI version (also caught by the same customManager, but with depName `uv`) is unaffected and keeps flowing through normal cadence.
Use pep440 versioning for the `python` image and `ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv` (which ships `python3.X-...` tags). Default `docker` versioning mis-orders pre-release/rc tags.
Dashboard category + extra labels — Python deps.
Dashboard category + extra labels — CI/infra.
